What Was Done: Transactions, orders, items, and section-level files were consolidated and standardized.
Reason: Different POS exports can have different formats and granularity.
Impact on the Project: Creates a consistent analytical base for forecasting.

What Was Done: Dates were converted into a consistent daily format.
Reason: Time-series models require a stable date index.
Impact on the Project: Allows daily aggregation and chronological train/test splitting.

What Was Done: Sales and transaction data were aggregated at daily level for Twisted Bar.
Reason: Forecasting was designed at short-term daily demand level.
Impact on the Project: Creates the target structure for SARIMAX and ML models.

What Was Done: Refund-related values and negative adjustments were identified and controlled.
Reason: Refunds can distort demand if interpreted as normal sales behavior.
Impact on the Project: Improves reliability of net sales and operational KPIs.

What Was Done: Missing or unavailable days were identified and tracked.
Reason: Gaps can bias time-series and rolling feature calculations.

What Was Done: The modeling calendar was completed and missing sales days were flagged.
Reason: SARIMAX and rolling validation require consistent temporal structure.
Impact on the Project: Prevents hidden gaps in the forecast timeline.

What Was Done: External weather data was aligned by date with POS data.
Reason: Weather must match the same day as restaurant operations.

What Was Done: Hourly weather signals were transformed into daily indicators such as cold, hot, rain, snow, fog, and haze/smoke hours.
Reason: Raw hourly weather is not directly compatible with daily sales modeling.
Impact on the Project: Turns weather into usable model features.
